title: The New PM, Claire Vo | Compile 26
summary: Claire Vo discusses how product management is shifting from managing engineering constraints to finding market fit and demand. As code generation becomes abundant, the bottleneck has moved from building to identifying what people want and convincing them to adopt it.
writeup: In this Compile 26 talk, Claire Vo argues that product management is moving away from managing engineering constraints and toward finding market fit and demand. The premise: as code generation becomes abundant, the bottleneck shifts from building software to identifying what people want and persuading them to adopt it. The takeaway for product work is a reweighting of priorities, with market discovery and demand validation taking precedence over the mechanics of shipping.
